title: Madangopal-jiu Temple
tagline: Madangopal-jiu Temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Madangopal Jiu Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Mellak, near Samta, Deulti, in Howrah district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
overview.description: Madangopal Jiu Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Mellak, near Samta, Deulti, in Howrah district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Samta, India
topPlaces.description: Samta (pronounced [ʃaːmtaː]) is a village and a gram panchayat in the Howrah district of West Bengal, India, on the banks of the Rupnarayan river. Samta is noted for being the home of Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay (namely, Sarat Chandra Kuthi) for twelve years, starting from the year 1923.

topPlaces.name: Barunda
topPlaces.description: Barunda is a census town in Bagnan II CD Block of Uluberia subdivision in Howrah district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Amalhara

topPlaces.name: Kolaghat (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Kolaghat (earlier referred to as Panskura II block) is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal. The first chairman of Kolaghat CD block was freedom fighter, teacher, social activist Late Shri Birbhadra Gauri from Saradabasan village, under Sagarbarh Gram Panchayat of Kolaghat CD Block.

topPlaces.name: Kolaghat

items.note: Samta (pronounced [ʃaːmtaː]) is a village and a gram panchayat in the Howrah district of West Bengal, India, on the banks of the Rupnarayan river. Samta is noted for being the home of Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay (namely, Sarat Chandra Kuthi) for twelve years, starting from the year 1923.
Barunda is a census town in Bagnan II CD Block of Uluberia subdivision in Howrah district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
Naupala is a census town in Bagnan II CD Block of Uluberia subdivision in Howrah district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
Amalhara is a census town in Kolaghat CD block in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

Kolaghat (earlier referred to as Panskura II block) is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal. The first chairman of Kolaghat CD block was freedom fighter, teacher, social activist Late Shri Birbhadra Gauri from Saradabasan village, under Sagarbarh Gram Panchayat of Kolaghat CD Block.
Kolaghat is a census town in Kolaghat CD Block, Tamluk subdivision of the Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Madangopal-jiu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Madangopal-jiu Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Madangopal-jiu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Madangopal-jiu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Madangopal-jiu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Madangopal-jiu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Madangopal-jiu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Madangopal-jiu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Samta, India, Barunda, Naupala, Amalhara, Kolaghat (community development block).
